19/104. An dèidh bhith aig a' phàirc, tha Bing a mothachadh nach eil Hoppity aige tuilleadh. After being at the park, Bing realises that he no longer has his Hoppity Voosh toy.
20/104. Tha Bing 's Flop a' cur romhpa a dhol air cuirm-chnuic ach tha an t-uisge a' tighinn. Bing and Flop prepare to go on a picnic, but then the rain arrives.
13/26. Seal organises a swimming contest in the lake. Owl decides to sign up.

programmes.show_name: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programmes.show_description: 6/52. Tha an t-Iasg-leòmhainn ag innse dhuinn mar a tha 'ad os cionn nan cuantan. The Lionfish tells us how they are the boss of the ocean.
...Adventures of Abney & Teal. 34/52. It's a sweltering day, much too hot to do anything apart from eat ice cream and swim.

13/13. Bheil Alpheus Benthos dha-rìribh air geataichean Lemuria a lorg? Has Alpheus Benthos really found the gates to Lemuria?
2/8. Tha rudan inntinneach aig an Speuradair Bekah ri innse dhuinn mun ghealaich. Astronaut Bekah shares some interesting facts about the moon.
...Ploc/The Rubbish World of Dave Spud. 1/26. The Spuds take the camper van on holiday and end up at the bottom of the sea.
...Ploc/The Rubbish World of Dave Spud. 2/26. Tha Pòl a' sàbhaladh aiseal a th' ann an cunnart shìos aig na docaichean. Dave rescues an endangered donkey from the docks.
6/6. The story of golf at Askernish in South Uist and professional coach Keil Beveridge gives us his weekly top playing tip.
26/26. Joy Dunlop has a familiar visitor in the studio and Calum Maclean is at Glen Affric National Nature Reserve to examine its heroic work - and this time he has company!

2/6. Gregor MacLeod uses traditionally smoked kippers to make Spanish croquettes, plus he takes eggs from his own hens for a pavlova tower.
...an Eòlais/The Disciple of Experience. 1/4. Leonardo da Vinci grows up in a Tuscan village surrounded by nature, then moves to Florence, where the Renaissance is in full bloom.

programmes.show_name: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programmes.show_description: 4/6. Tha na seòid a' cleachdadh greideal phraisich gus grunn rudan luath is furasta a dhèanamh. The lads use an iron hotplate to create a variety of quick and easy meals.
An insight into life on the islands of Lewis, Uist and Skye from the BBC TV series Around Scotland, originally broadcast in 1970.
